title: Drawing Adventure Exercise
author: Jessica Young
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.r72d9qe

steps.title: Begin by lightly drawing a small...
steps.instruction: Begin by lightly drawing a small circle- about the size of your thumb.

steps.title: On the top left side of...
steps.instruction: On the top left side of the small circle, lightly draw on a small oval. The oval should begin in the circle and point out of the circle at about a 70-80 degree angle.

steps.title: Repeat step 2, but mirrored onto...
steps.instruction: Repeat step 2, but mirrored onto the right side such that you end with 2 small ovals pointing out of the circle that are mirror images vertically of one another.

steps.title: At the bottom of the small...
steps.instruction: At the bottom of the small circle, lightly draw on another larger circle about 1.3 times the size of the smaller one.

steps.title: Erase the bottom of the first...
steps.instruction: Erase the bottom of the first small circle such that it is now only connected to the larger circle by 2 points.

steps.title: Erase where the small ovals on...
steps.instruction: Erase where the small ovals on either side of the first circle overlap with the first small circle.

steps.title: Now that you have one outside...
steps.instruction: Now that you have one outside line, go over all of the outside line with your pencil while making small curly flourishes- aka continiually write w to achieve this.

steps.title: In the middle of the original...
steps.instruction: In the middle of the original small circle, lightly draw in an isoscles trapezoid (the pizza hut logo shape)- make sure the edges are rounded though and not sharp!

steps.title: On the bottom left and bottom...
steps.instruction: On the bottom left and bottom right corners of the trapezoid, draw small circles to cover the points.

steps.title: Inside the trapezoid, on the middle...
steps.instruction: Inside the trapezoid, on the middle of the right half, draw and fill in an oval at 180 degrees.

steps.title: Inside the trapezoid, on the middle...
steps.instruction: Inside the trapezoid, on the middle of the left half, draw a small n, elongated on either side. Horizontally, this should line up with the top of the oval you just drew.

steps.title: In the very center of the...
steps.instruction: In the very center of the trapezoid vertically slightly under the n and oval, draw a small horizontal line. This should not tough the n nor the oval, and should be almost under both.
